claim_short: 2601.15784 Ansorena Marcos Remarks about symmetry-type conditions of conditional bases asks whether a spreading Markushevich basis can fail to be a Schauder basis. Full negative answer: every spreading Markushevich basis in a Banach space is Schauder. Proof uses source facts that spreading bases are seminormalized and equivalent to subsequences, Rosenthal ell_1 theorem, Bessaga-Pelczynski weakly-null selection,...
notes: registry_status=full | review_focus=Review should check the use of the source definition of a spreading basis, specifically that the shift to a subsequence is an isomorphism onto the closed subsequence span. Review should also check the standard non-trivial weak-Cauchy selection step. With...
packet_title: Full Result: Spreading Markushevich Bases in Banach Spaces are Schauder
source_question: Source paper: J. L. Ansorena and A. Marcos, *Remarks about symmetry-type conditions of conditional bases of Banach spaces*, arXiv:2601.15784. Location: page 16, after Theorem 3.6 and the discussion of the summing basis of c0. Question: whether a spreading Markushevich basis that fails to be a Schauder basis exists.
source_title: Remarks about symmetry-type conditions of conditional bases of Banach spaces
